root_text.sn44.11:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sabhiyo kaccāno ñātike viharati giñjakāvasathe.
root_text.sn44.11:1.2: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yenāyasmā sabhiyo kaccāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sabhiyena kaccānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.11:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako āyasmantaṁ sabhiyaṁ kaccānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.11:1.4: “kiṁ nu kho bho, kaccāna, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.11:1.7: “Kiṁ pana, bho kaccāna, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.11:2.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho kaccāna, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.11:2.3: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.11:2.4: “Kiṁ pana, bho kaccāna, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.11:2.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.11:3.1: “‘Kiṁ nu kho, bho kaccāna, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.sn44.11:3.4: ‘Kiṁ pana, bho kaccāna, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.sn44.11:3.6: na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vadesi.
root_text.sn44.11:3.7: ‘Kiṁ nu kho, bho kaccāna, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.sn44.11:3.9: hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vadesi.
root_text.sn44.11:3.10: ‘Kiṁ pana, bho kaccāna, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.sn44.11:3.12: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vadesi.
root_text.sn44.11:3.13: Ko nu kho, bho kaccāna, hetu, ko paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ samaṇena gotamenā”ti?
root_text.sn44.11:3.14: “Yo ca, vaccha, hetu, yo ca paccayo paññāpanāya rūpīti vā arūpīti vā saññīti vā asaññīti vā nevasaññīnāsaññīti vā, so ca hetu, so ca paccayo sabbena sabbaṁ sabbathā sabbaṁ aparisesaṁ nirujjheyya.
root_text.sn44.11:3.15: Kena naṁ paññāpayamāno paññāpeyya rūpīti vā arūpīti vā saññīti vā asaññīti vā nevasaññīnāsaññīti vā”ti.
root_text.sn44.11:3.18: “Yassapassa, āvuso, etamettakena ettakameva tampassa bahu, ko pana vādo evaṁ abhikkante”ti.

translation_text.sn44.11:1.1: At one time Venerable Sabhiya Kaccāna was staying at Ñātika in the brick house.
translation_text.sn44.11:1.2: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to him, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.11:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to Sabhiya Kaccāna:
translation_text.sn44.11:1.4: “Worthy Kaccāna, does a realized one still exist after death?”
translation_text.sn44.11:1.5: “Vaccha, this has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.11:1.7: “Well then, does a realized one no longer exist after death?”
translation_text.sn44.11:1.8: “This too has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.11:2.1: “Well then, does a realized one both exist and not exist after death?”
translation_text.sn44.11:2.2: “This has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.11:2.4: “Well then, does a realized one neither exist nor not exist after death?”
translation_text.sn44.11:2.5: “This too has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.11:3.1: “Worthy Kaccāna, when asked these questions, you say that this has not been declared by the Buddha.
translation_text.sn44.11:3.13: What’s the cause, what’s the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.11:3.14: “In order to describe him as ‘formed’ or ‘formless’ or ‘percipient’ or ‘non-percipient’ or ‘neither percipient nor non-percipient’, there must be some cause or reason for doing so. But if that cause and reason were to totally and utterly cease without anything left over,
translation_text.sn44.11:3.15: how could you describe him in any such terms?”
translation_text.sn44.11:3.16: “Worthy Kaccāna, how long has it been since you went forth?”
translation_text.sn44.11:3.18: “One who has learned this much in this time, reverend, has learned a lot, who can say what lies ahead!”
translation_text.sn44.11:4.6: The Linked Discourses on undeclared questions are complete.
translation_text.sn44.11:5.7: The Book of the Six Sense Fields is finished.

variant_text.sn44.11:3.18: bahu, ko pana vādo evaṁ → ko pana vādo eva (bj, pts1ed)
